title: memecylon subquadrangulare
intro: Memecylon subquadrangulare, a species of plant in the family Melastomataceae, is commonly known as the "Kerala forest tree." This plant is native to the Western Ghats region of India, and its bark, leaves, and roots are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The medicinal properties of Memecylon subquadrangulare include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which provide relief from fever, wounds, and skin infections. The health benefits of using this plant include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing protection against infections, and it can be prepared into herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and topical creams.
